text: The patient is positioned in the prone jackknife position, and the surgeon identifies the internal and external openings of the anal fistula.
The surgeon performs a thorough debridement of the fistula tract, and the internal opening is marked with a suture.

The surgeon inserts a flexible cannula sheet into the external opening of the fistula, advancing it to the internal opening. A paste is injected through the cannula, and the internal opening is closed with multiple sutures.
The surgeon closes the external opening of the fistula with a single loose suture, securing the area while maintaining some flexibility.
